by_judge.judge: P SREE SUDHA IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 24.06.2026

narrative:
On June 24, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 43.4%, with judges addressing 1,090 of the 2,509 scheduled cases. This represents a decline in clearance efficiency compared to the previous day, which saw a 47.6% hearing rate when 1,173 of 2,465 cases were heard.

Among individual benches, S. Vishwajith Shetty managed the highest single-list volume, hearing 91 of 249 scheduled cases (36.5%). High-efficiency performances were noted by Vijaykumar A. Patil, who cleared 75.5% of his primary list (40 of 53 cases), and Chillakur Sumalatha, who resolved 76.6% of a 64-case list (49 heard). Conversely, the bench of the Chief Justice & K.S. Hemalekha showed polarized results, converting 86.2% of a 29-case list but only 25.4% of a larger 59-case docket.

Stage-wise analysis reveals that cases listed for Orders dominated court schedules with 775 instances, achieving a 48.6% conversion rate—a slight drop from yesterday's 50.1%. Admissions dropped more sharply, falling from 49.2% yesterday to 39.1% today (189 of 483 cases). Preliminary Hearings achieved a 41.6% hearing rate (200 of 481 cases), while Final Hearings remained the slowest stage, converting only 22.4% (86 of 384 cases) compared to yesterday's 24.5%.

Regarding case-type distribution, Writ Petitions (WP) remained the largest category with 1,290 scheduled and 546 heard (42.3%), closely matching yesterday's 42.5% rate. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) saw their clearance rate decrease to 42.6% (170 of 399 cases) from yesterday's 49.2%. Similarly, Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) fell to 44.9% from 52.3% on the prior day. In contrast, Criminal Appeals (CRL.A) demonstrated stronger progress, converting 61.4% of their 88 scheduled cases.
